summary:
Third-generation cephalosporins with specific efficacy include only cefoperazone for treating pseudomonal infection due to its documented effectiveness versus other agents lacking this property directly stated by medical references provided herein. Agent focused primarily on identifying which option meets both criteria—being from the appropriate generation AND actively combating \( Pseudomonas aeruginosa \). 

End summary. ```plaintext
The critical point is that only cefoperazone among given options has been shown to be active against Pseudomonas aeruginosa, distinguishing it as an essential treatment when dealing with such bacteria. This conclusion stems solely from evidence supporting cefoperazone's unique capability compared to others not specified or less potent toward these particular organisms.
``` Summary:

Only cefoperazone among available third-generation cephalosporins demonstrates activity against \( Pseudomonas aeruginosa \); thus, it stands out uniquely capable based on current clinical guidelines and research findings highlighted above. End summary. ```
```

This concise version captures the essence without extraneous details but maintains clarity regarding why cefoperazone was selected over alternatives.
